articles.title: Odisha Schools Closed Till June 20 Due to Heatwave; Classes to Resume on June 22 in Five Districts - Jagran Josh
articles.full_text:
Odisha Schools Closed Till June 20 Due to Heatwave; Classes to Resume on June 22 in Five Districts
Odisha schools in five districts will remain closed till June 20 due to extreme heatwave conditions. Check affected districts, reopening date, revised timings, and latest updates here.
Odisha government extends closure of schools, anganwadi centers in 5 districts till June 20, 2026, in view of prevailing heatwave conditions in the state with temperatures touching over 42°C in some parts. Schools in Bolangir, Sonepur, Ganjam, Angul and Sundargarh will reopen on June 22 as June 21 is a Sunday. The decision has been taken to protect children from harsh weather conditions as India Meteorological Department (IMD) predicts hot conditions to continue till June 21.
Odisha Schools Summer Vacation Extended
The state government has announced an extension of the summer vacation for schools and anganwadi centres in five heatwave-affected districts. Initially scheduled to reopen on June 18, educational institutions in these districts will now remain closed till June 20 due to the severe rise in temperature.
Districts Where Schools Will Remain Closed
The extended holiday applies to the following districts:
Heatwave Conditions Continue Across Odisha
Several parts of Odisha continue to experience extreme heat, with Boudh recording 42.7°C, while Sambalpur, Hirakud, Jharsuguda, Sonepur, and Titlagarh also reported temperatures above 40°C. The IMD has forecast that heatwave conditions are likely to persist until June 21, prompting authorities to extend the school closure.
Revised School Timings in Nuapada District
While schools remain closed in the five districts, authorities in Nuapada district have opted for modified school timings instead of a complete closure. Classes will be conducted from 6:30 AM to 11:30 AM between June 18 and June 20 to help students avoid peak daytime temperatures.
Apeksha Agarwal is an Education Journalist with over 3.5 years of experience. She covers a wide range of topics, including school board examinations, entrance tests, admissions, results, scholarships, and higher education updates. Over the years, she has closely tracked major examinations such as JEE Main, NEET UG, CUET, and various state-level entrance exams, helping students stay informed throughout their academic journey. Apeksha has a Master's degree in Journalism and Mass Communication and a certificate in Digital Journalism. She is passionate about transforming complex educational developments into clear, accessible, and useful information. Her reporting focuses on providing students, parents, and educators with accurate updates and practical insights on examinations, results, and policy changes. She believes that quality education journalism can make a meaningful difference by helping students make informed decisions about their future.

articles.title: Schools in Angul, Sonepur to Reopen on June 22 as Heatwave Persists - Dailyhunt
articles.full_text:
Even as the state government had set June 18 as the reopening date, at least two Odisha districts have extended summer vacations, citing dangerous heat conditions.
The Angul district administration announced Wednesday that all government and private schools will remain shut until June 20, with classes resuming only on June 22.
The District Education Officer issued the directive to protect students from extreme temperatures, even as monsoon conditions have offered some relief in other parts of the state. The district administration has urged parents to ensure children remain hydrated and are kept away from direct sunlight.
Sonepur has also ordered the closure of Anganwadi centres, schools, colleges and all educational institutions until June 20. Examinations scheduled during this period will go ahead as planned, with authorities directing that they be held preferably in the morning to shield students from peak afternoon heat.
The summer break in Odisha began in late April following a sharp rise in temperatures and the state government had extended it before announcing June 18 as the reopening date.
